title: Nauclea aralioides
taxonomy: Nauclea aralioides is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is native to tropical Africa. The plant is classified as a member of the Rubiaceae family, which includes over 600 genera and 13,000 species. Within the Rubiaceae family, Nauclea aralioides is placed in the tribe Naucleeae, along with other similar species. This classification reflects the plant's unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other members of the Rubiaceae family.
uses: Nauclea aralioides has been traditionally used in various medicinal practices. Its bark is used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
